 {"id":"bd-503","title":"Implement gum_spin()","description":"**Spinner with fallback**

## What
Function to show a spinner during operations with fallback to simple output.

## Why
Spinners provide feedback during potentially long operations. Without gum, we show static messages.

## Implementation
```bash
gum_spin() {
    local title="$1"
    shift
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" && "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]]; then
        gum spin --spinner dot --title "$title" -- "$@"
    else
        [[ "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]] && echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} $title" >&2
        "$@"
    fi
}
```

## Note on Usage
Not heavily used in v1 since we show per-repo progress. May be useful for operations like 'Checking for updates...'

```bash
print_banner() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return

        echo -e "${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}" >&2
    fi
}
```

-{"id":"bd-51fm","title":"Implement repo_preflight_check()","description":"# Repository Preflight Checks

## Parent Epic: bd-cpxq (Preflight Safety Checks)

## Purpose
Validate repo is in safe state before invoking agent.

## Implementation

```bash
repo_preflight_check() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON=""

    # Is it a git repo?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="not_a_git_repo"
        return 1
    fi

    # Git identity configured? (ADDED: prevents commit failures)
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.email 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_email_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.name 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_name_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi

    # Shallow clone? (ADDED: some operations may fail)
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/shallow" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="shallow_clone"
        return 1
    fi

    # Dirty submodules? (ADDED: complex state)
    if git -C "$repo_path" submodule status 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^+'; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="dirty_submodules"
        return 1
    fi

    # Rebase in progress?
    if [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-apply" ]] || [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-merge" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="rebase_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Merge in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/MERGE_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="merge_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cherry-pick in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="cherry_pick_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Detached HEAD?
    local branch
    branch=$(git -C "$repo_path" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$branch" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="detached_HEAD"
        return 1
    fi

    # Has upstream?
    local upstream
    upstream=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --abbrev-ref "@{u}" 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$upstream" ]] && [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_PUSH_STRATEGY:-push}" != "none" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="no_upstream_branch"
        return 1
    fi

    # Diverged from upstream?
    if [[ -n "$upstream" ]]; then
        local ahead behind
        read -r ahead behind < <(git -C "$repo_path" rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ "$ahead" -gt 0 ]] && [[ "$behind" -gt 0 ]]; then
            PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diverged_from_upstream"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Unmerged paths?
    if git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --unmerged 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="unmerged_paths"
        return 1
    fi

    # git diff --check clean?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" diff --check &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diff_check_failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Too many untracked files?
    local untracked_count
    untracked_count=$(git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    if [[ "$untracked_count" -gt "${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_UNTRACKED:-1000}" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="too_many_untracked_files"
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}
```

## Skip Reason to User Action Mapping

| Reason | User Action |
|--------|-------------|
| git_email_not_configured | Run: git config user.email "you@example.com" |
| git_name_not_configured | Run: git config user.name "Your Name" |
| shallow_clone | Run: git fetch --unshallow |
| dirty_submodules | Fix submodule state first |
| rebase_in_progress | Complete or abort rebase |
| merge_in_progress | Complete or abort merge |
| detached_HEAD | Switch to a branch |
| no_upstream_branch | Set upstream tracking branch |
| diverged_from_upstream | Pull and rebase first |
| too_many_untracked_files | Review .gitignore, clean untracked |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 preflight conditions checked
- [ ] Skip reasons are human-readable
- [ ] User action mapping covers all skip reasons
- [ ] Tests cover all skip scenarios

## Parent Epic: bd-3ov0 (Installer Integration)

## Changes to install.sh

### 1. Add ntm Detection
```bash
check_ntm_installed() {
    command -v ntm &>/dev/null
}

get_ntm_version() {
    ntm --version 2>/dev/null | head -1
}
```

### 2. Add Interactive Prompt
```bash
prompt_ntm_install() {
    [[ "${RU_NON_INTERACTIVE:-}" == "1" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "yes" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "no" ]] && return 1
    
    cat << EOF
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│          Enable ntm integration?                            │
│                                                             │
│   Enables: ru agent-sweep                                   │
│   Provides: AI-assisted commit and release automation       │
│                                                             │
│   [Y] Yes (recommended)                                     │
│   [n] No, skip for now                                      │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
EOF
    read -r response
    [[ "$response" =~ ^[yY]$ ]] || [[ -z "$response" ]]
}
```

### 3. Add ntm Installation
```bash
install_ntm() {
    if check_ntm_installed; then
        log_info "ntm already installed: $(get_ntm_version)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_step "Installing ntm..."
    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash
}
```

### 4. Integration Flow
After ru install:
1. Check tmux (warn if missing)
2. Prompt for ntm (respects env vars)
3. Install ntm if requested
4. Verify installation

 {"id":"bd-5iwb","title":"Implement file size limits and binary detection","description":"# File Size and Binary Detection

## Parent Epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails & Validation)

## Purpose
Prevent committing large files or unexpected binaries.

## File Size Limit

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB="${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB:-10}"

is_file_too_large() {
    local file="$1"
    local max_bytes=$((AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB * 1024 * 1024))
    local size
    
    # Cross-platform stat
    size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    [[ "$size" -gt "$max_bytes" ]]
}
```

## Binary Detection

```bash
is_binary_file() {
    local file="$1"
    # file command returns "... text" for text files
    \! file -b "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qiE "(text|script|json|xml|empty)"
}
```

## Integration

Check during validate_commit_plan():
1. For each file in plan
2. If file exists and is too large: block
3. If file is binary without explicit allow: block

## Configuration

- --max-file-mb=N: Override default 10MB limit
- AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS: Patterns for allowed binaries

## Allowed Binary Patterns

Some binaries are expected (icons, fonts):
```
*.png
*.jpg
*.gif
*.ico
*.woff
*.woff2
```

## Error Messages

- "File too large: path/to/file (15MB > 10MB limit)"
- "Binary file without explicit allow: path/to/binary"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:53:39.776937603-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:53:39.776937603-05:00"}

-{"id":"bd-6kme","title":"Implement portable JSON parsing (json_get_field)","description":"# Portable JSON Parsing Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Create json_get_field() function that works on systems without jq using layered fallbacks.

## Implementation

```bash
json_get_field() {
    local json="$1" field="$2"
    
    # Best: jq (most reliable)
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: python3
    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
        python3 -c "
import json,sys
field=sys.argv[1]
data=json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
v=data.get(field,'')
print(v if isinstance(v,(str,int,float,bool)) else json.dumps(v))
" "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: perl with JSON::PP
    if command -v perl &>/dev/null && perl -MJSON::PP -e1 2>/dev/null; then
        perl -MJSON::PP -0777 -ne '...' "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: minimal sed (flat strings only, fragile)
    sed -nE 's/.*"'"$field"'":[[:space:]]*"([^"]*)".*/\1/p' <<<"$json" | head -n1
}
```

## Also Implement
- json_is_success() - Check if JSON has success:true
- json_escape() - Escape string for JSON embedding (backslash, quotes, newlines, tabs)

## Testing
- Test with jq available
- Test with only python3
- Test with only perl
- Test with only sed (minimal fallback)
- Test nested objects (should return JSON for non-primitives)

## Considerations
- sed fallback is fragile, only works for simple flat strings
- Always try to use jq/python when available
- Document limitations in code comments","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:48:37.5200696-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:48:37.5200696-05:00"}

```bash
    local json="$1" field="$2"
    
    # Best: jq (most reliable)
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: python3
    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
        python3 -c "
import json,sys
field=sys.argv[1]
data=json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
v=data.get(field,'')
print(v if isinstance(v,(str,int,float,bool)) else json.dumps(v))
" "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: perl with JSON::PP
    if command -v perl &>/dev/null && perl -MJSON::PP -e1 2>/dev/null; then
        perl -MJSON::PP -0777 -ne '...' "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: minimal sed (flat strings only, fragile)
    sed -nE 's/.*"'"$field"'":[[:space:]]*"([^"]*)".*/\1/p' <<<"$json" | head -n1
}
```

## Also Implement
- json_is_success() - Check if JSON has success:true
- json_escape() - Escape string for JSON embedding (backslash, quotes, newlines, tabs)

- Test with only sed (minimal fallback)
- Test nested objects (should return JSON for non-primitives)

- Always try to use jq/python when available

## Background

The NTM integration plan specifies support for per-repo .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json files that can override default agent-sweep behavior. This enables repository maintainers to:

- Customize file denylist patterns

## Function to Implement

### load_repo_agent_config()

Purpose: Load and merge per-repo configuration with global defaults.

Location: ~/.config/ru/agent-sweep.yml (global) merged with repo/.ru-agent.yml

Config Schema:
```yaml
# Enable/disable for this repo
enabled: true

# Override file patterns to deny
file_denylist_extra:
  - "*.generated.ts"
  - "vendor/*"

# Override file size limit (bytes)
max_file_size: 5242880  # 5MB

# Skip phases
skip_phases:
  - release  # Skip phase 3 for this repo

# Custom understanding context
extra_context: |
  This repo uses a monorepo structure.
  Database migrations are in db/migrations/.

# Hooks (run in repo context)
pre_sweep_hook: "./scripts/pre-agent.sh"
post_sweep_hook: "./scripts/post-agent.sh"
```

2. Parse YAML/JSON using portable approach (yq -> python3 -> simple parser)

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Create a Claude Code session for a repository via ntm robot mode.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_spawn_session() {
    local session="$1"
    local workdir="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-60}"
    local output
    
    # Spawn with wait-for-ready
    if output=$(ntm --robot-spawn="$session" \
        --spawn-cc=1 \
        --spawn-wait \
        --spawn-dir="$workdir" \
        --ready-timeout="${timeout}s" 2>&1); then
        echo "$output"
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        echo "$output"
        return $exit_code
    fi
}
```

## ntm Flags Used
- --robot-spawn=SESSION: Create session with given name
- --spawn-cc=1: Launch 1 Claude Code agent
- --spawn-wait: Wait for agents to be ready
- --spawn-dir=PATH: Set working directory
- --ready-timeout=Ns: Maximum wait for ready state

## Response Schema (on success)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "session": "ru_sweep_myrepo_12345",
  "created_at": "2025-01-06T15:30:00Z",
  "working_dir": "/data/projects/myrepo",
  "agents": [
    {
      "pane": "0.0", "type": "user", "ready": true
    },
    {
      "pane": "0.1", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 2500
    }
  ],
  "layout": "tiled",
  "total_startup_ms": 2500
}
```

## Error Handling
- Exit 0: Session created successfully
- Exit 1: Error (check error_code in JSON)
- RESOURCE_BUSY: Session already exists (kill and retry)
- TIMEOUT: Ready timeout exceeded

## Implementation

```bash
save_agent_sweep_state() {
    local status="$1"
    local state_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
    local tmp_file="${state_file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Prefer jq/python for safe JSON generation
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -n --arg run_id "$RUN_ID" --arg status "$status" \
            --argjson completed "$(printf "%s\n" "${COMPLETED_REPOS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)" \
            "{run_id:\$run_id,status:\$status,repos_completed:\$completed}" > "$tmp_file"
    # ... fallbacks for python3, manual JSON
    fi
    
    mv "$tmp_file" "$state_file"
}

load_agent_sweep_state() {
    local state_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
    [[ \! -f "$state_file" ]] && return 1
    
    RUN_ID=$(json_get_field "$(cat "$state_file")" "run_id")
    COMPLETED_REPOS=($(json_get_field "$(cat "$state_file")" "repos_completed" | jq -r ".[]"))
    # ...
    return 0
}

filter_completed_repos() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local filtered=()
    for repo in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        if \! array_contains COMPLETED_REPOS "$repo"; then
            filtered+=("$repo")
        fi
    done
    repos_ref=("${filtered[@]}")
}

cleanup_agent_sweep_state() {
    rm -f "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/state.json"
}
```

SAFE_BASH_COMMANDS (always allowed):
  git, grep, find, ls, cat, make, npm, cargo, go, python, pytest,

BLOCKED_COMMANDS (never allowed):

Special gh handling:

  BLOCKED in Plan mode: gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr merge

```bash
node_modules

dist/
build/

.DS_Store
```

## Implementation

```bash
# Check if file matches denylist
# Args: $1=file_path (relative)
# Returns: 0=denied, 1=allowed
is_file_denied() {
    local file="$1"
    for pattern in $AGENT_SWEEP_DENYLIST_PATTERNS; do
        case "$file" in
            $pattern) return 0 ;;  # Denied
        esac
    done
    return 1  # Allowed
}
```

 {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Check if ntm is installed and functional before attempting agent-sweep.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_check_available() {
    if ! command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        return 1  # Not installed
    fi
    # Verify robot mode works (fast check)
    if ! ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null; then
        return 2  # Installed but not functional
    fi
    return 0  # Available and functional
}
```

## Return Values
- 0: ntm available and functional
- 1: ntm not installed
- 2: ntm installed but robot mode not working

## Usage in cmd_agent_sweep
```bash
local ntm_status
ntm_check_available
ntm_status=$?
if [[ $ntm_status -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_error "ntm is not installed. Install with:"
    log_error "  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash"
    return 3
elif [[ $ntm_status -eq 2 ]]; then
    log_error "ntm is installed but robot mode is not working."
    log_error "Try: ntm --robot-status"
    return 3
fi
```

## Considerations

 {"id":"bd-y3vd","title":"Implement has_release_workflow() detection","description":"# Release Workflow Detection

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Determine if repo should have release automation (Phase 3).

## Implementation

```bash
has_release_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local workflows_dir="$repo_path/.github/workflows"
    
    # Check explicit per-repo config first
    local repo_config="$repo_path/.ru/agent-sweep.conf"
    if [[ -f "$repo_config" ]]; then
        source "$repo_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Check user-level per-repo config
    local repo_name=$(basename "$repo_path")
    local user_config="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/agent-sweep.d/${repo_name}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$user_config" ]]; then
        source "$user_config"
        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
            never) return 1 ;;
            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Use gh API if available
    if command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
        local remote_url
        remote_url=$(git -C "$repo_path" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ -n "$remote_url" ]] && gh workflow list -R "$remote_url" 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "release\|deploy\|publish"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # Fallback: check workflow files
    [[ -d "$workflows_dir" ]] || return 1
    grep -riqE "(on:|tags:|release:|workflow_dispatch:)" "$workflows_dir"/*.yml 2>/dev/null
}
```

## Release Strategy Values
- never: No releases
- auto: Agent proposes, ru validates (default)
- tag-only: Create tag, no GH release
- gh-release: Tag + GH release + monitor Actions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:57:21.4043331-05:00"}
